topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. My carefully crafted piece, the one I’d poured months of sleepless nights into, was under a different name. A name that belonged to some unknown, opportunistic soul. A slow, cold burn started in my chest, spreading outward until my hands clenched into tight fists on the desk. I stared at the screen, the words mocking me with their misplaced attribution. I didn't scream; the silence in my small apartment was far more terrifying. I started a meticulous inventory of every contact, every shared document, tracing the digital breadcrumbs until I found the source, a ghost in the machine. The plan began to form, sharp and precise, like a surgeon preparing for an incision.

[story 2]

When the editor finally called, the words tasted like ash. My submission, my voice, had been hijacked. A bitter, acidic feeling rose in my throat, making me swallow hard. I looked at the printed proof, the unfamiliar author’s signature glaring back at me. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I didn't call the editor back immediately. Instead, I spent the next twenty-four hours quietly gathering evidence, cross-referencing dates, analyzing publication patterns. Every misplaced comma, every shared file, became a weapon in my arsenal. A slow, deliberate smile touched my lips—a gesture devoid of warmth. It was a look of pure, focused intent.

[story 3]

The realization hit me during my morning coffee ritual. The headline was undeniably mine, the analysis, the core argument—all mine—but the byline belonged to a stranger. A sudden, intense heat flooded my face, quickly replaced by a chilling calm. I set the mug down with deliberate slowness, the clink of ceramic echoing in the quiet kitchen. This wasn't just a professional slight; it felt like a personal affront, a deliberate erasure. I walked to my computer, my steps measured and heavy. The air around me seemed to thicken with unspoken resolve. I began drafting a response, not a plea, but a meticulously constructed counter-narrative, designed to expose the deception with surgical accuracy.
